target.name: Innoviz Technologies
target.founder: Omer Keilaf, Oren Rosenzweig, Amit Steinberg, Oren Buskila

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Innoviz Technologies (INVZ) is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The valuation universe for this company is constrained: while five private peers operate in the same sector—Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—no direct public comparables exist in this space. This absence of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers within the sector significantly limits traditional peer-median triangulation.

Given the composition of the available data, peer-median methodology is not applicable. The five identified private peers lack sufficient homogeneity in stage, scale, or valuation tier to produce a meaningful narrow peer set. A cross-sector peer median would risk introducing material bias by conflating Drones & Autonomous economics with broader technology or aerospace comparables, which operate under fundamentally different unit economics, regulatory frameworks, and capital requirements.

This analysis cannot proceed to a specific valuation range without additional data on comparable company financials (revenue, EBITDA, burn rates), transaction multiples from recent private or public financings in the sector, or forward-looking metrics for Innoviz itself. The scarcity of public comps and the heterogeneity of the private peer set represent the primary valuation uncertainty. Resolution would require either: (i) public market comparables from adjacent aerospace or autonomy subsectors, or (ii) transaction-level data from recent funding rounds or M&A activity among peers.
